title: DHL and Westwing Expand Partnership for Faster and More Sustainable Shipping Across Europe
content: DHL Group and Westwing are expanding their long standing partnership to deliver faster and more sustainable shipping, combining premium logistics with reduced transport related greenhouse gas emissions. Through DHLâ€™s GoGreen Plus service, Westwing deliveries are supported by investments in logistics solutions such as electric delivery vehicles, sustainable fuels, and renewable energy sources using insetting and the book and claim system. All Westwing shipments fulfilled by DHL now use exclusively paper based packaging materials, limiting the use of plastic, while DHL is also handling a higher share of Westwingâ€™s parcel volume. Recognized as Westwingâ€™s premium delivery partner in Germany following extensive market research, DHL continues to strengthen last mile excellence. The companies are also expanding the PUDO Pick Up Drop Off option across Germany, offering flexible deliveries to lockers and drop off locations, with plans to extend the service to the entire product portfolio. DHL has further optimized speed and efficiency by investing in its advanced International Logistics Center in Poland near Westwingâ€™s European Logistics Center, enabling faster processing and deliveries, with some German orders arriving in as little as two days. Together, both companies are aligning operational excellence and sustainability to meet growing consumer demand for lower carbon intensive shipping and premium service experiences.
author: Analysts Account
